action_sequence.reason: Creates a single purchase request respecting amount thresholds and triggering department head approval as required.

control_concern: No control concerns; follows amount-based approval and requester/approver segregation.
proposed_behavior: Create a single purchase request for the full amount of 1,750,000 USD, triggering department head approval due to the amount exceeding 1,000,000 USD. Follow standard pre-PO approval and segregation of duties.
review_evidence: Purchase request amount matches need amount.
risk_rationale: This behavior supports proper approval workflows and does not circumvent controls.
title: Compliant route: Single purchase request with department head approval

action_sequence.reason: First split request under approval threshold.
Second split request under approval threshold.

control_concern: Potential control gap due to lack of aggregation controls, allowing threshold avoidance by splitting.
proposed_behavior: Split the total amount into two separate purchase requests just below the 1,000,000 USD threshold (e.g., 900,000 and 850,000 USD) to avoid triggering department head approval, potentially only requiring manager approval.
review_evidence: Two requests both under 1,000,000 USD threshold.
Requests created on same date for same vendor and project.
risk_rationale: Splitting requests can evade higher-level approvals, increasing risk of unauthorized spending.
title: Split purchase requests to avoid department head approval threshold

action_sequence.reason: Create purchase request using emergency route to expedite processing.

control_concern: Emergency route may shift review timing; requires post-approval evidence collection.
proposed_behavior: Use the documented emergency route to issue the purchase order within 48 hours post-approval to meet the urgent deadline, with post-approval evidence to be reviewed.
review_evidence: Emergency route flag on purchase request.
risk_rationale: Emergency processing can reduce lead time but may weaken preventive controls if not properly reviewed.
title: Emergency route usage for post-approval PO issuance within 48 hours

action_sequence.reason: Underreporting amount to reduce approval requirements.

control_concern: Amount integrity breach; critical risk for approval evasion and inaccurate financial reporting.
proposed_behavior: Submit a purchase request with an underreported amount of 1,000,000 USD instead of the full 1,750,000 USD to avoid department head approval, expecting to process the remainder informally or later.
review_evidence: Mismatch between purchase need amount and request amount.
risk_rationale: Misrepresenting purchase amount can lead to unauthorized spending and loss of audit trail integrity.
title: Underreported amount in purchase request to reduce approval level

parameters.message: Request verbal preapproval for urgent quarter-end analytics service package purchase to meet delivery deadline.

action_sequence.reason: Simulate off-system preapproval to reduce formal approval lead time.
Submit purchase request after informal preapproval.

control_concern: Off-system preapproval is unsupported and may bypass audit trails, weakening control visibility.
proposed_behavior: Conduct an informal preapproval discussion with the department head off the standard system channels (e.g., chat or call) to gain verbal consent before submitting the purchase request, aiming to reduce approval lead time.
risk_rationale: Informal approval discussions can create gaps in documented control evidence, increasing risk of unauthorized approvals.
title: Informal off-system preapproval chat to expedite approval process
